**Cristian Andres Palomeque Valoyes** (born 2 April 1994) is a Colombian [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who plays for [Honduran Liga Nacional de Ascenso](/wiki/Honduran_Liga_Nacional_de_Ascenso "Honduran Liga Nacional de Ascenso") club [Juticalpa F.C.](/wiki/Juticalpa_F.C. "Juticalpa F.C.")

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

### Alianza Petrolera

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Alianza Petrolera")]

#### 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2012")]

Palomeque joined [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera") on loan from [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and made his debut on 20 October as a starter, scoring a goal in a 2–1 victory against [Universitario Popayán](/wiki/Universitario_Popay%C3%A1n "Universitario Popayán") for the [2012 Categoría Primera B](/wiki/2012_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B_season "2012 Categoría Primera B season").[[3]](#cite_note-3) He made his second season appearance against [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali"), netting a double in a 4–1 triumph at the [Pascual Guerrero](/wiki/Estadio_Ol%C3%ADmpico_Pascual_Guerrero "Estadio Olímpico Pascual Guerrero") just nine days later.[[4]](#cite_note-4) On 4 November, he scored against [Atlético Bucaramanga](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Bucaramanga "Atlético Bucaramanga") in a 1–0 victory.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Palomeque would score against Bucaramanga once more, this time in a 2–0 home win.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In a decisive match against [Deportivo Rionegro](/wiki/Deportivo_Rionegro "Deportivo Rionegro"), Palomeque scored the second goal in a 3–1 victory, thus allowing Petrolera to dispute the promotion play-off against [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Despite having only played a handful of games, Palomeque's goal scoring abilities became an important factor for Petrolera as they reached their most important moment in history. In the first leg of the final, Palomeque netted what would be his most vital goal yet, helping Petrolera top América 2–1.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Him along with his team, failed to find the net in the second leg, with América eventually tying up the series at 2-2. However, he still managed to score in the penalty shoot-out which Petrolera remarkably won 4–3, therefore being promoted.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

#### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2013")]

After taking part in the Colombian team that won the [2013 South American Youth Championship](/wiki/2013_South_American_Youth_Championship "2013 South American Youth Championship"), Palomeque made his first appearance in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A#Torneo_Apertura "Categoría Primera A") against Atlético Nacional.[[10]](#cite_note-10) On 20 April, Palomeque scored his first top division goal from a free kick in a 2–2 draw against [Deportivo Cali](/wiki/Deportivo_Cali "Deportivo Cali").[[11]](#cite_note-11) He successfully converted a goal from the penalty spot on 12 May against [Cúcuta Deportivo](/wiki/C%C3%BAcuta_Deportivo "Cúcuta Deportivo"). Petrolera, however, lost the match 2–3.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Against [Atlético Huila](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Huila "Atlético Huila"), Palomeque scored the sole goal in a 1–0 victory.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

#### Once Caldas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Once Caldas")]

On 24 January 2014, Palomeque joined [Once Caldas](/wiki/Once_Caldas "Once Caldas") on loan from Atlético Nacional.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) He scored his first goal for the club in the 93rd minute in a 1–1 draw against [Boyacá Chicó](/wiki/Boyac%C3%A1_Chic%C3%B3_F.C. "Boyacá Chicó F.C.").[[16]](#cite_note-16) His only other goal with Once Caldas was also scored against Boyacá Chicó in a 4–1 victory at the [Estadio Palogrande](/wiki/Estadio_Palogrande "Estadio Palogrande").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

#### San Antonio Scorpions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: San Antonio Scorpions")]

On 18 February 2015, he joined [NASL](/wiki/North_American_Soccer_League_(2011%E2%80%932017) "North American Soccer League (2011–2017)") club [San Antonio Scorpions](/wiki/San_Antonio_Scorpions "San Antonio Scorpions") on loan.[[18]](#cite_note-18) He scored the first goal in a preseason friendly against [MLS](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer") side [FC Dallas](/wiki/FC_Dallas "FC Dallas"), that resulted in a 2–1 victory for his club.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

#### Jaguares de Cordoba

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Jaguares de Cordoba")]

After a short unsuccessful spell in the United States, Palomeque joined [Jaguares de Córdoba](/wiki/Jaguares_de_C%C3%B3rdoba "Jaguares de Córdoba") for the second half of the [2015 Torneo Finalización](/wiki/2015_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2015 Categoría Primera A season").[[20]](#cite_note-20) During his tenure at Jaguares, he only scored 2 goals in a 4–3 defeat against [La Equidad](/wiki/La_Equidad "La Equidad").[[21]](#cite_note-21)

#### Colón

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Colón")]

On 7 January 2016, he joined [Argentine Primera División](/wiki/Argentine_Primera_Divisi%C3%B3n "Argentine Primera División") club [Colón](/wiki/Club_Atl%C3%A9tico_Col%C3%B3n "Club Atlético Colón") on loan from Atlético Nacional.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

#### Real Cartagena

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Real Cartagena")]

Palomeque joined [Real Cartagena](/wiki/Real_Cartagena "Real Cartagena") on loan, on 6 January 2017.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

### Later career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Later career")]

In late 2017, Palomeque joined [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera_F.C. "Alianza Petrolera F.C.") for the [upcoming season](/wiki/2018_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2018 Categoría Primera A season").[[25]](#cite_note-25) On December 10, 2018, Palomeque joined [La Equidad](/wiki/La_Equidad "La Equidad").[[26]](#cite_note-26) In January 2020, Alianza Petrolera announced his return for a third spell.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

On 14 January 2021, Palomeque joined [Ecuadorian Serie A](/wiki/Ecuadorian_Serie_A "Ecuadorian Serie A") club [Mushuc Runa](/wiki/Mushuc_Runa_S.C. "Mushuc Runa S.C.").[[28]](#cite_note-28) After a year without a club, on 5 February 2023, he joined [Honduran Second Division](/wiki/Honduran_Liga_Nacional_de_Ascenso "Honduran Liga Nacional de Ascenso") club [Juticalpa](/wiki/Juticalpa_F.C. "Juticalpa F.C.").[[29]](#cite_note-29) His team reached the promotion final against [Génesis](/w/index.php?title=Club_Deportivo_G%C3%A9nesis&action=edit&redlink=1 "Club Deportivo Génesis (page does not exist)") and lost it in a penalty shootout 10–9, after a 2–2 draw. Palomeque hit the crossbar on his first attempt and had his second attempt saved by the keeper.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cristian_Palomeque&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Honours")]

### Club
